jokes.text: Oh, no. The firing tie.
jokes.setup: Hermes mentions conducting performance reviews 'with a twist'
jokes.punchline: Fry recognizes the ominous significance of Hermes' necktie choice
jokes.explanation: Strong economy and specificity - the idea of having a dedicated 'firing tie' is absurd and character-appropriate for bureaucrat Hermes

jokes.text: and the lowest-rated employee, possibly Zoidberg, will be fired at sundown. Zoidberg.
jokes.setup: Hermes explains the firing process in bureaucratic terms
jokes.punchline: He immediately assumes it will be Zoidberg, then directly addresses him
jokes.explanation: Good character integration - Hermes' casual cruelty toward Zoidberg is well-established

jokes.text: Good luck, everybody but Zoidberg.
jokes.setup: Standard workplace encouragement expected
jokes.punchline: Hermes specifically excludes Zoidberg from his good wishes
jokes.explanation: Builds on the established Hermes-Zoidberg dynamic with casual cruelty

jokes.text: Uh, yes, sir, I will be sure to snore that science lamp right away.
jokes.setup: Amy describes assisting the Professor with important research
jokes.punchline: Her malapropism reveals she wasn't paying attention and misheard instructions
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate - Amy's ditzy nature comes through in the nonsensical task

jokes.text: Now, Leela, couldn't we just fire you and have Fry or Bender fly the ship? / Not if you stand on their air hose much longer.
jokes.setup: Hermes questions Leela's necessity as pilot
jokes.punchline: Leela points out Hermes is literally standing on Fry/Bender's air supply
jokes.explanation: Strong visual comedy setup with perfect economy - the visual punchline completely undermines Hermes' question

jokes.text: My job? Toilets 'n' boilers, boilers 'n' toilets. Plus that one boiling toilet.
jokes.setup: Scruffy describes his maintenance duties in his typical laconic style
jokes.punchline: The escalation to 'one boiling toilet' - an absurd hybrid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy line - laconic delivery, absurd escalation, highly quotable. The rhythm and repetition make it memorable

jokes.text: while many of you do half-ass jobs and the rest do jobs whose ass ratio ranges from 42% to a mere 11%
jokes.setup: Hermes begins a typical bureaucratic speech about employee performance
jokes.punchline: He quantifies 'half-ass' work with precise percentages, treating it as a measurable metric
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character integration - Hermes' bureaucratic nature applied to crude workplace humor with precise metrics

jokes.text: And that employee is... Hermes Conrad.
jokes.setup: Hermes builds up to firing the worst employee after evaluating everyone
jokes.punchline: He fires himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with strong character logic - Hermes' bureaucratic thoroughness leads to self-destruction

jokes.text: My performance review proves the main drag on our profit is the time I waste on performance reviews.
jokes.setup: Hermes explains his firing decision
jokes.punchline: His bureaucratic system has identified itself as the problem
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor that's perfectly in character - bureaucracy defeating itself through its own logic

jokes.text: They'll be here two seconds ago.
jokes.setup: Hermes mentions notifying the central bureaucracy
jokes.punchline: Time travel bureaucracy - they arrive before being called
jokes.explanation: Clever sci-fi bureaucracy joke with perfect economy - maximum absurdity in minimal words

jokes.text: I suppose you're here to tell me to pack it in? / Only if you buy me dinner first.
jokes.setup: Hermes assumes the bureaucrat is there for official business
jokes.punchline: The bureaucrat's flirtatious response completely subverts expectations
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection but not particularly character-specific or memorable

jokes.text: I'm mark 7-G, the machine who's replacing you. / I'm mark 7-G, the machine who's replacing you.
jokes.setup: Professor introduces the replacement robot
jokes.punchline: Robot immediately repeats the exact same line, showing its limited programming
jokes.explanation: Simple repetition joke that establishes the robot's limitations but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Will you be conducting an exit interview? / Well, I wouldn't say no to a little friendly debriefing.
jokes.setup: Robot asks about standard exit interview procedure
jokes.punchline: Professor's response has sexual undertones with 'friendly debriefing'
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo that fits Professor's character but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Bureaucrat Conrad, you are ordered to relieve yourself of duty. / Sounds like a party.
jokes.setup: Official order to end Hermes' employment
jokes.punchline: Hermes interprets 'relieve yourself' in bathroom humor context
jokes.explanation: Predictable bathroom humor pun - functional but not clever

jokes.text: So, I hear you're a machine... ow! Traitor!
jokes.setup: Fry starts to flirt with the robot replacement
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately hits him for being inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Fry's inappropriate behavior and Leela's quick correction

jokes.text: My friend's ample tuchus is barely out the door, and you're already schmoozing up to his replacement?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg defends Hermes against Fry's behavior
jokes.punchline: Uses Yiddish 'tuchus' (butt) and 'schmoozing' - unexpected cultural reference from alien character
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Zoidberg's loyalty, plus unexpected Yiddish vocabulary

jokes.text: I don't think Hermes really liked you. In fact, he hated you more than anyone ever hated anyone.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg expresses sadness about losing Hermes
jokes.punchline: Fry's blunt statement about Hermes' extreme hatred, delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of harsh truth - Fry's oblivious honesty

jokes.text: What, you mean the remarks and the hits? That was just friend teasing.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg is told Hermes hated him
jokes.punchline: He interprets verbal and physical abuse as friendly teasing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg delusion - his desperate need for friendship makes him reinterpret abuse

jokes.text: He was the only one who cared enough to insult me. / I insult you, you fat sack. / Sure, when it's convenient.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg explains that Hermes' insults showed he cared
jokes.punchline: Bender offers an insult but Zoidberg dismisses it as not genuine enough
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Zoidberg's standards for friendship insults are very specific

jokes.text: On the bright side, I'll never see Zoidberg again.
jokes.setup: Hermes contemplates being fired and leaving Planet Express
jokes.punchline: His main comfort is escaping Zoidberg
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - even in crisis, Hermes' hatred for Zoidberg remains his priority

jokes.text: Then I guess this wouldn't be a good time to tell you that I'm pregnant. / What?! / And you're not the father. / No! / I'm just joking, husband.
jokes.setup: Hermes is already stressed about losing his job
jokes.punchline: LaBarbara piles on fake pregnancy news, then fake infidelity, then reveals it's a joke
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with multiple fake-outs building tension then relief

jokes.text: See now? Things don't seem so bad any more, do they? / You're right. Thank you, Labarbara. / Oh, also, I wrecked the car.
jokes.setup: LaBarbara's fake pregnancy scare makes Hermes feel better about his situation
jokes.punchline: Just as he's feeling grateful, she drops real bad news
jokes.explanation: Good timing joke - the relief is immediately undercut by actual bad news

jokes.text: Well played.
jokes.setup: LaBarbara serves extremely spicy curried goat
jokes.punchline: Hermes acknowledges her tactical use of spice after initially complaining
jokes.explanation: Good married couple humor - acknowledging the tactical deployment of painful spice

jokes.text: Ah. Fire. / Aah! It burns! It burns! / Hmm, not bad. Needs a little hot sauce.
jokes.setup: Hermes tastes LaBarbara's extremely spicy curried goat
jokes.punchline: After screaming in pain, he casually suggests it needs more spice
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment that will pay off later - Hermes' extreme spice tolerance

jokes.text: This here is a mugging! Hand over your skin. / My ebony splendor? Me don't think so.
jokes.setup: Roberto demands something valuable in a mugging
jokes.punchline: He wants skin instead of money, and Hermes refers to his own skin as 'ebony splendor'
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist premise with great character voice - Hermes' pride in his appearance

jokes.text: Ain't nothing crazy about me but my brain. / Right, brain? / Right! No, not you, right brain. / Right, left brain? Right!
jokes.setup: Roberto denies being crazy
jokes.punchline: He has a conversation with both sides of his brain, getting confused about which is responding
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific insanity - Roberto's split-brain dialogue is perfectly nuts

jokes.text: I can't! When I fight machinery, machinery always wins!
jokes.setup: LaBarbara tells Hermes to do something about the robot attacker
jokes.punchline: Hermes admits his philosophical weakness against machines
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific cowardice that sets up his later cybernetic enhancement arc

jokes.text: You're under arrest for attempted epidermicide.
jokes.setup: Police arrest Roberto for his crimes
jokes.punchline: They create a pseudo-scientific term for 'trying to steal skin'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay creating a medical-sounding term for an absurd crime

jokes.text: That's why you're going straight to the electromagnetic chair. / And then I'm-a stab it. Ha-haw!
jokes.setup: Officer mentions futuristic version of electric chair
jokes.punchline: Roberto threatens to stab the chair itself, showing his illogical thinking
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Roberto's nonsensical aggression toward inanimate objects

jokes.text: S-s-stop! It's driving me sane!
jokes.setup: Roberto is being executed in the electromagnetic chair
jokes.punchline: Instead of driving him crazy, the chair is making him sane - his worst nightmare
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion - for a crazy robot, sanity is torture. Perfect economy and character logic

jokes.text: Hey, Bender, you should become an executioner. You could kill humans and wear a cool hood. / Nah, I like my victims to know who did it.
jokes.setup: Fry suggests Bender would enjoy being an executioner
jokes.punchline: Bender prefers personal recognition for his murders
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - his ego requires credit for his evil deeds

jokes.text: Fry, could you come in here and help me rub on this new scented body oil? / In a second. I'm eavesdropping. / Please, Fry? Leela punishes me when I don't use enough. / I said not now!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg asks for help with intimate personal care
jokes.punchline: The TMI details about Leela punishing him and Fry's complete disinterest
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Zoidberg's inappropriate oversharing and Fry's priorities

jokes.text: Pops?! / Is that a harpoon in your chest? / Yes. And I'm happy to see you.
jokes.setup: Son is shocked by Hermes' cybernetic modification
jokes.punchline: Hermes treats having a harpoon surgically implanted as casual, references 'happy to see you' trope
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to absurd situation, with callback to common greeting phrases

jokes.text: And the best part is, the whole package fits neatly in the space where my giblets used to be.
jokes.setup: Hermes shows off his new harpoon implant
jokes.punchline: He refers to his removed organs as 'giblets' like he's poultry
jokes.explanation: Good absurd word choice - treating human organs like turkey parts

jokes.text: I'm kind of a harpoon snob, and let me say, that thing is gorgeous.
jokes.setup: Son reacts to father's chest harpoon
jokes.punchline: He evaluates it like a connoisseur of harpoons
jokes.explanation: Funny absurdist concept - being a harpoon expert in casual conversation

jokes.text: Pitiful and sickening. / Okay, little man, let a professional bureaucrat show you how it's done.
jokes.setup: Robot fails at a simple task
jokes.punchline: Hermes dismisses the advanced robot and claims bureaucratic superiority
jokes.explanation: Good character pride moment - Hermes' professional ego vs. automation

jokes.text: Welcome back, old friend. / I missed you terribly. / You do everything terribly. And I'm not your friend.
jokes.setup: Hermes warmly greets Zoidberg upon returning
jokes.punchline: Immediately returns to insulting him, crushing the warm moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - Hermes can't help but return to his default insult mode

jokes.text: Good old Hermes. When he stops insulting, that's when I worry.
jokes.setup: Hermes just insulted Zoidberg harshly
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg finds comfort in the abuse, seeing it as normal friendship
jokes.explanation: Great callback to earlier theme about Zoidberg valuing Hermes' consistent abuse

jokes.text: 'Taint a boiler nor a toilet. Pass.
jokes.setup: Scruffy is asked to help with teeth stuck in garbage disposal
jokes.punchline: He refuses because it doesn't fall under his strictly defined job duties, plus accidental innuendo with 'taint'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy moment - rigid job definition plus accidental double entendre. Extremely quotable

jokes.text: I don't want to end up a cold, emotionless machine like you. / That's sweet, Hermes.
jokes.setup: Professor insults Hermes by calling him a cold, emotionless machine
jokes.punchline: Hermes takes it as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Hermes' bureaucratic nature makes him appreciate being called machine-like

jokes.text: I have golf game in half-hour, so... half-hour.
jokes.setup: Hermes asks how long extendo-arm surgery will take
jokes.punchline: Back-alley surgeon's schedule determines the operation time
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the surgeon's casual, unprofessional approach

jokes.text: I don't know. It seems like a lot of trouble to cut off your arm just to snake a drain. / Ah-ah-ah, it's not just a drain snake.
jokes.setup: Professor questions the logic of amputation for a simple task
jokes.punchline: Hermes reveals it's actually a multi-purpose tool, justifying the extreme surgery
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Hermes' growing obsession with practical upgrades

jokes.text: A machine that can bend? No way.
jokes.setup: Hermes shows off his pipe-bending attachment
jokes.punchline: Bender, whose name literally means 'one who bends,' is amazed by this concept
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - Bender is literally a bending robot amazed by bending capability

jokes.text: Such a mild insult. That's not my Hermes.
jokes.setup: Hermes gives a relatively gentle put-down
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg notices the insult wasn't harsh enough and finds this concerning
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Zoidberg has standards for how Hermes should treat him

jokes.text: I was going to donate them to the salvation air force, but they're starting to rot.
jokes.setup: Question about what happened to Hermes' discarded body parts
jokes.punchline: Futuristic version of Salvation Army, but the body parts are spoiling
jokes.explanation: Decent futuristic wordplay but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Whoo-whoo-whoo-whoo, whoo-whoo-whoo-whoo! Nyah-ah-ah.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg takes Hermes' discarded body parts
jokes.punchline: Makes gleeful noises suggesting he's going to do something with them
jokes.explanation: Sets up later plot point but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: 'Oh, mon, I'm Hermes, and I think it stinks in here like a green snake, or something else that's Jamaican.'
jokes.setup: Zoidberg is puppeteering a mini-Hermes made from body parts
jokes.punchline: His terrible impression includes random Jamaican stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Zoidberg's poor understanding of Hermes

jokes.text: Good one at my expense, Hermes.
jokes.setup: Mini-Hermes just insulted Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg compliments his own puppet's insult of himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent pathetic moment - Zoidberg praising abuse he's giving himself

jokes.text: And it will cut two or three hours a day off my cornrowing routine.
jokes.setup: Hermes gets robotic hair implants
jokes.punchline: He's optimizing his elaborate hairstyle maintenance with technology
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific joke about Hermes' signature hairstyle and efficiency obsession

jokes.text: You know, you look even hotter in infrared.
jokes.setup: Hermes gets a cybernetic eye upgrade
jokes.punchline: Uses his new infrared vision to compliment his wife
jokes.explanation: Decent innuendo that makes practical use of his new cybernetic feature

jokes.text: Husband, some things were not meant to be cylonned.
jokes.setup: LaBarbara reacts to Hermes' cybernetic eye
jokes.punchline: She uses 'Cylon' as a verb, referencing the robot race from Battlestar Galactica
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi reference turned into verb form - creative and quotable

jokes.text: Nice, shiny metal ass, Hermes.
jokes.setup: Hermes has replaced his rear end with robotic parts
jokes.punchline: Bender compliments it, referencing his own signature 'shiny metal ass' catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Bender's signature phrase applied to Hermes' situation

jokes.text: I still have one thing no robot shall ever have... a good old flesh-and-blood human brain. / Yes, a big, smart brain. Not like dum-dum Zoidberg, right, Hermes? Right?
jokes.setup: Hermes declares his brain makes him superior to robots
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg eagerly agrees and tries to bond over shared superiority, missing the irony of calling himself dumb
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Zoidberg's desperate need for acceptance makes him insult himself

jokes.text: Not like dum-dum Zoidberg, right, Hermes? Right?
jokes.setup: Hermes praising intelligence
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg tries to get Hermes to insult him by providing the setup
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pathetic moment - Zoidberg literally begging to be insulted

jokes.text: Mecha-Hermes has no interest in such nonsense.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg tries to connect with Hermes about brain superiority
jokes.punchline: Hermes coldly dismisses him, referring to himself in third person as 'Mecha-Hermes'
jokes.explanation: Good character evolution - Hermes becoming more robotic in personality as well as body

jokes.text: Of course, I tried eating you, but your flesh was too spicy.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg is questioned about what he's doing with Hermes' body parts
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to attempting cannibalism but was deterred by the seasoning
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that pays off the earlier spicy food setup - Zoidberg's casual admission is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Thanks to years of Labarbara's curried goat, no doubt. / But my mecha-stomach has no need of goat.
jokes.setup: Discussion of why Hermes' flesh was too spicy to eat
jokes.punchline: Hermes credits his wife's cooking, then robotically dismisses his need for food
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Hermes' continuing transformation into machine thinking

jokes.text: 'Yeah, from restaurant to restaurant. Ho ho, mon.'
jokes.setup: Mini-Hermes is told that Zoidberg has taken up running
jokes.punchline: The puppet delivers a fat joke in a stereotypical Jamaican accent
jokes.explanation: Standard fat joke with stereotypical accent - functional but not clever

jokes.text: 'Seriously, though, have you lost weight?' / Yes. / 'Looks like you found it again.'
jokes.setup: Puppet asks if Zoidberg lost weight, gets hopeful response
jokes.punchline: Immediately delivers crushing comeback about finding the weight
jokes.explanation: Classic weight joke structure - predictable but well-executed timing

jokes.text: 'I'm not your friend. I'm out of here the second you let go of my bowels.'
jokes.setup: Zoidberg is enjoying his puppet friendship with mini-Hermes
jokes.punchline: The puppet delivers harsh truth about being made from Hermes' intestines
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about the puppet's literal construction with perfect Hermes-style cruelty

jokes.text: I can only see one of his four mouth-tendrils moving.
jokes.setup: Amy watches Zoidberg's ventriloquism act
jokes.punchline: Comments on his poor technique by noting his alien anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Zoidberg's alien physiology affecting his performance

jokes.text: Regret that I still have one human part. That meat puppet disgusts me.
jokes.setup: Hermes watches his own body parts being puppeteered
jokes.punchline: Instead of nostalgia, he's disgusted by his former human flesh
jokes.explanation: Good character development - Hermes' complete rejection of his humanity

jokes.text: Robot brain implant? Never. No one in right mind would do that.
jokes.setup: Professor refuses to help with brain surgery
jokes.punchline: Claims no sane person would get a robot brain while Hermes is clearly planning to
jokes.explanation: Standard irony setup but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Ah, a freshly dug robot grave. Let's exhume-a-zoom-zoom.
jokes.setup: Hermes finds a robot cemetery to steal a brain
jokes.punchline: Makes up a cheerful rhyming phrase for grave robbing
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but the rhyme feels forced

jokes.text: Bing-reka! The brain circuit.
jokes.setup: Hermes successfully finds a robot brain to steal
jokes.punchline: Combines 'bingo' and 'eureka' into one exclamation
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau word but not particularly funny in context

jokes.text: Enjoy eternity without your brain, anonymous dead robot.
jokes.setup: Hermes steals the brain from a robot grave
jokes.punchline: Coldly dismisses the victim of his theft
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hermes' complete lack of empathy post-cybernetics

jokes.text: 'I'm ready to shoot myself if it'll get me off your lap.'
jokes.setup: Zoidberg asks if mini-Hermes is ready to watch brain surgery
jokes.punchline: The puppet would rather commit suicide than continue being held by Zoidberg
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that perfectly captures Hermes' attitude toward Zoidberg

jokes.text: Oh, I hope to God I'm using that word correctly.
jokes.setup: LaBarbara threatens divorce proceedings in emotional speech
jokes.punchline: Breaks character concern to worry about her word choice
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor that undercuts dramatic moment with realistic self-doubt

jokes.text: Your wife's moving plea has made me realize you might have litigious survivors.
jokes.setup: Professor seems moved by LaBarbara's emotional appeal
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's only concerned about potential lawsuits, not ethics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor character moment - appears moral but is actually just selfish and practical

jokes.text: There will be no noodles until the operation is complete.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions wanting to try Korean noodles
jokes.punchline: Robotic Hermes makes an absurdly dramatic declaration about noodle prohibition
jokes.explanation: Good robotic character voice - makes trivial things sound important

jokes.text: Maybe not, but perhaps I can encourage you with my encouragement drill.
jokes.setup: Professor refuses to perform surgery
jokes.punchline: Hermes threatens torture with a euphemistically named device
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with bureaucratic doublespeak - torture renamed as 'encouragement'

jokes.text: You? With those clumsy claws? / It's true, old friend. My claws are too clumsy for such delicate work. / 'But mine aren't.'
jokes.setup: Hermes dismisses Zoidberg's surgical abilities
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg agrees but reveals his puppet (made from Hermes' parts) will operate
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot twist with perfect logic - Hermes' own hands are more skilled than anyone else's

jokes.text: ♪ I was lonesome for my friend a minute ago ♪ / ♪ when a happy thought dispelled my woe ♪
jokes.setup: Zoidberg begins explaining his motivation through song
jokes.punchline: The overly theatrical musical explanation for a horrifying surgical procedure
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between cheerful musical style and disturbing surgical context

jokes.text: ♪ and I felt perhaps he'd be a bit less dull ♪ / ♪ if I sliced up his cyborg skull ♪
jokes.setup: Zoidberg's cheerful song about friendship
jokes.punchline: Reveals his solution involves violent brain surgery, sung sweetly
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - violent surgery described as friendship improvement in cheerful song

jokes.text: Does anyone else find it freaky that Zoidberg is singing harmony with himself?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg performs elaborate musical number
jokes.punchline: Amy points out the impossible vocal arrangement
jokes.explanation: Good meta-observation about the musical impossibility but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Sweet reawakenin'... Of me, a Jamaican-in.
jokes.setup: Hermes awakens from surgery back to his normal self
jokes.punchline: Makes a forced rhyme about his Jamaican identity
jokes.explanation: Forced rhyme that signals Hermes' return to his normal personality - character-appropriate but weak wordplay

jokes.text: if you can't be man enough with your own parts, no amount of machinery will help.
jokes.setup: Hermes explains what he learned from his cybernetic experience
jokes.punchline: The moral lesson with potential double entendre about 'being man enough with your own parts'
jokes.explanation: Good thematic resolution with subtle innuendo - the lesson applies both literally and metaphorically

jokes.text: I'm gonna peel off your skin and eat it like pencil shavings.
jokes.setup: Roberto continues threatening the family
jokes.punchline: Specific disturbing imagery comparing skin to pencil shavings
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Roberto's skin fixation with disturbing specific imagery

jokes.text: Thank God Zoidberg didn't reattach my nerves.
jokes.setup: Hermes is being attacked and tortured by Roberto
jokes.punchline: He's grateful for Zoidberg's medical incompetence for the first time
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - Zoidberg's incompetence finally helps someone, and Hermes appreciates him for once

jokes.text: The spicy curried goat. Hermes' skin is so saturated with it that it's dissolving the bad robot.
jokes.setup: Roberto is defeated after trying to eat Hermes' skin
jokes.punchline: The extreme spiciness of his diet makes his flesh toxic to robots
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to multiple setups - Hermes' spice tolerance and LaBarbara's extreme cooking become the solution

jokes.text: In the end, all his implants were no match for my wife's reckless seasoning.
jokes.setup: Roberto is defeated by Hermes' spicy skin
jokes.punchline: Hermes credits his wife's 'reckless seasoning' as more powerful than cybernetic enhancements
jokes.explanation: Perfect thematic resolution - his wife's natural cooking beats artificial enhancement, with great phrase 'reckless seasoning'

jokes.text: Zoidberg, I... I know we have never been friends, / but I appreciate what you did. Thank you. / Never been friends? Ouch! What a zinger.
jokes.setup: Hermes tries to give a sincere thank you to Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Even his gratitude contains an insult, which Zoidberg appreciates as a good joke
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - even Hermes' gratitude is accidentally cruel, and Zoidberg loves it

jokes.text: You acted like a big jerk. You were selfish and stupid, and you don't deserve a wife like Labarbara or a friend like me.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg is encouraged to deliver a comeback zinger
jokes.punchline: His 'zinger' is just blunt honest criticism with no wit
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Zoidberg's idea of a 'zinger' is just honest, heartfelt criticism

jokes.text: It's funny because it's mean.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg delivers harsh but honest criticism to Hermes
jokes.punchline: He explains his comedy theory while misunderstanding what makes something funny
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about comedy itself, showing Zoidberg's fundamental misunderstanding of humor
